parsed_review.summary: CP00-647 is a descriptor-only RP21 Admin Console slice (Test/Golden Case Set M07.S07-S22, Hermes Evidence Packet M08, Claude Review Packet M09.S01-S02; 40 units, risk B). Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claim is supported and correctly scoped: manifest reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, and production_ready_flag=admin_console_test_evidence_review_packet_descriptor_verified (a descriptor-verification flag, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim). Distribution matches the baseline exactly: deliverables {implementation:16, ui:6, claude_review:2, failure_recovery:4, test:8, contract:2, security_audit:2} and micro-phase rows M07=16/M08=22/M09=2, consistent across review-baseline, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, registry ADMIN_CONSOLE_CP647_REQUIREMENTS, and the generated contract. The M07 split is clean (CP646 covered S01-S06 prechecks; CP647 picks up S07 happy-path onward, 22 rows total, no gap/overlap). Boundary enforcement is real, not just asserted: validateAdminConsoleCp647TestEvidenceReviewPacketDescriptor (validators.js) checks every row for descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / raw_payload_included=false, requires all 33 false-flags (writes_product_state, executes_*_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, emits/persists_audit_event, executes_golden_case_runtime, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, exposes/persists_review_payload, leak_detected, permission_bypass_detected, etc.), the no-leak guards (incl. deny_over_allow_permission_baseline and no_real_tenant_matter_document_or_billing_data), promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, runtime_case_execution_opened=false, hermes_runtime_receipt_emitted=false, review_payload_persisted=false, and the CP00-648/RP21.P02.M09.S03 handoff; model.test.js exercises these and the validator script asserts valid=true plus contract deep-equality and runtime_opened=false. Authority boundary is respected: Claude is read-only/non-final-approver (claude_final_approval_claimed=false everywhere, human_final_approval_required=true), Hermes is descriptor-only (hermes_runtime_opened=false, runtime_receipt_emitted=false, not embedded in product runtime). No-write/no-real-data holds (synthetic_only=true, no_real_data=true). Review-hardening pipeline is clean: readiness auth preflight passed with well-formed JSON, claude-review-result has empty valid_review_receipts and invalid_review_attempts (no auth failure, 0-byte, malformed, fenced, or tool-call-shaped output passed off as valid). Plan live_state, next-pack-queue (live_latest=CP00-647, next=CP00-648@RP21.P02.M09.S03), and contract current/historical bindings agree. Two non-blocking P3 advisories noted below; no P0/P1/P2. The expected human final-approval / enterprise-trust authority lies outside this receipt and the pack does not claim it, so goal-closeout is not blocked by that boundary.

findings.title: source_descriptor_ref pack_id/production_ready_flag are hardcoded and could drift from a non-default source_descriptor
findings.details: In createAdminConsoleCp647TestEvidenceReviewPacketDescriptor, source_descriptor_ref.descriptor is read dynamically from the passed-in sourceDescriptor, but pack_id and production_ready_flag are hardcoded to ADMIN_CONSOLE_CP646_PACK_BINDING. If a caller ever passed input.source_descriptor that is not the CP646 descriptor, the ref would become internally inconsistent (descriptor name from one pack, pack_id from CP646). Every in-repo call site (validate-rp21-admin-console-contract.mjs, createAdminConsoleCoreContractProjection, model.test.js) passes the CP646 descriptor or uses the default, so the generated contract and all validations are correct today; the CP646 descriptor uses the same hardcoding pattern, so behavior is consistent across packs.
findings.recommendation: Optionally derive source_descriptor_ref.pack_id/production_ready_flag from sourceDescriptor.pack_binding, or assert the source descriptor matches the upstream pack, so the ref cannot silently drift. Non-blocking; no action required for CP00-647 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Advisory only. Latent and unreachable via any current call path; does not affect descriptor outputs, validation results, or boundary enforcement. No fix required for closeout.

findings.title: Final closeout still requires the full validation matrix and exactly one valid pack-level review receipt
findings.details: command-evidence.json records node --check syntax checks on the six implementation files plus `npm run rp21:admin-console:validate` (all exit 0). The broader closeout matrix (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet recorded, and claude-review-result.json shows exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false with status=pending_review. manifest/command-evidence/construction-inspection all correctly remain in_progress/PENDING_REVIEW with production_ready=false, so this is the expected pre-review state rather than a defect. As a read-only reviewer I cannot execute these commands.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping status to closed, run the full validation matrix and normalize this review into a single valid hardened receipt (exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run must become true). Keep production_ready=false until the descriptor-verification gate is satisfied; do not weaken any gate.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected for an in_progress descriptor pack; the closeout harness runs the full matrix and persists the receipt after this verdict. Non-blocking for the descriptor review itself; setting it as a blocker would deadlock the review-then-close sequence.
